page: Amazon Web Services r5.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server instance. It is powered by two Intel Xeon 8175M processors, providing 48 physical cores and 96 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The instance features 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, but it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the r5.metal demonstrates strong multi-core performance and memory bandwidth. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance is rated as strong at 81,870.14, and the Passmark CPU score reaches 57,080.9. Memory bandwidth tests show strong results, with read-only small block cached speeds at 4,545.56 GB/sec and large block uncached speeds at 222.69 GB/sec. Database operations are highly efficient, as indicated by a Passmark database score of 21,974.43 and a Redis SET throughput of 74.16 million operations per second.

While the server excels in memory-intensive and multi-threaded tasks, single-core performance is moderate, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1,207.0 and a stress-ng single-core score of 1,450.99. Additionally, LLM text generation performance is a notable bottleneck, with the 7B model achieving only 5.25 tokens per second. Given the lack of local storage and GPUs, this instance is best suited for large in-memory databases, high-throughput web serving, and memory-intensive enterprise applications that rely on network-attached storage.
description: Amazon Web Services r5.metal is a memory-optimized bare-metal server featuring 96 vCPUs across 48 physical Intel Xeon 8175M cores running at 3.1 GHz. Equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, it offers a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB, making it highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads. The instance lacks local storage and GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. Benchmark data shows strong multi-core CPU performance and exceptional memory bandwidth, with Redis SET operations reaching 74.16 million operations per second and static web serving throughput at 42.63 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is average, and LLM text generation speeds are weak. This server is best suited for large-scale in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring direct hardware access without virtualization overhead.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5.metal is a bare-metal server with 96 vCPUs and 768 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity bare-metal platform delivering extensive DDR4 memory and strong multi-core processing for demanding in-memory database workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical Intel Xeon cores running at 3.1 GHz
768.0 GB of high-speed DDR4 memory (8.0 GB per core)
Strong memory bandwidth and multi-core CPU benchmark performance
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Excellent fit for large in-memory databases and Redis caching layers
